REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MARSHALL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. CONTINUED TO APRIL 12, 2019. BY NO LATER THAN MARCH 29, 2019 PLAINTIFF SHALL FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF LISTING ANY CASES THAT ARE FACTUALLY SIMILAR TO THE CASE AT BAR, SPECIFICALLY A DISMISSAL OF AN ACTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE IN THE FACE OF A PENDING ANTI-SLAPP FOLLOWED BY FILING OF A NEW COMPLAINT, INCLUDING THE SAME CAUSES OF ACTION AND EXCLUDING "SLAPPABLE" ALLEGATIONS. PLAINTIFF MAY ACCOMPANY EACH CITATION BY A ONE PARAGRAPH (NOT TO EXCEED 20 LINES) EXPLANATION/ARGUMENT ADDRESSING WHY THE CASE IS ON POINT AND FACTUALLY SIMILAR. IF PLAINTIFF FAILS TO LOCATE ANY SUCH CASES, THE SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF SHOULD STATE SO. BY NO LATER THAN APRIL 8, 2019 DEFENDANT MAY FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSE BRIEF ADDRESSING THE CASES CITED BY PLAINTIFF, LIMITING THE ARGUMENT TO ONE PARAGRAPH (NOT TO EXCEED 20 LINES) PER CASE. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MARSHALL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. SOUTH SUTTER, LLC V. LJ SUTTER PARTNERS, L.P. (2011) 193 CAL.APP.4TH 634. PLAINTIFF IS NOT PROHIBITED FROM FILING THIS ACTION. DEFENDANT MAY FILE AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION IF APPROPRIATE. ORDER FOR THE SAME SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.. JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

DECLARATION OF PATRICK A. HORMILLOSA IN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT, AND REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO CCP 426.16 (ANTI-SLAPP) (TRANSACTION ID # 100075744) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MONGALO, MOISES VILLARROEL, ELIZABETH MORALES, ELIZABETH

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MARSHALL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE COURT HAS PREVIOUSLY WARNED THE DEFENDANT TO ONLY FILE THE ANTI-SLAPP IF APPROPRIATE. DEFENDANT FILED THIS MOTION WITHOUT PROVIDING ANY EXPLANATION FOR ITS DECISION TO NOT FILE A TIMELY ANTI-SLAPP OR PROVIDING ANY BASIS FOR THE COURT TO EXERCISE ITS DISCRETION TO ALLOW A LATE FILING. ADDITIONALLY, WHILE DEFENDANT'S MOTION HEAVILY RELIES ON SUTTER, DECIDED PRIOR TO SIGNIFICANT CHANGE IN ANTI-SLAPP LAW, THERE IS NO DISCUSSION OF BARAL AND ITS PROGENY ON APPLICABILITY OF SUTTER. BARAL V. SCHNITT (2016) 1 CAL.5TH 376. THE COURT DOES NOT SUGGEST THAT SUTTER IS NO LONGER GOOD LAW. HOWEVER, SUTTER'S RELIANCE ON "GRAVAMEN" OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION WARRANTED A DISCUSSION. SEE RUTTER-GUIDE, CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL 7:772. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

Off Calendar Aug-16-2019 Set For Order To Show Cause On Oct-22-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Patrick Hormillosa, Esq., 415-362-2529, appearing for Moises Mongalo, Plaintiff and opposing party

Mark Wilson, Esq., 415-217-6990, appearing for Walter Worsham, for Defendant and moving party.

ARGUED. Mr. Wilson requested that the court deny the motion without prejudice to allow him to review the case law cited. The Court questioned why the defendant filed a demurrer and why did he wait so long.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MARSHALL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is DENIED. The Court has previously warned the Defendant to only file the ANTI-SLAPP if appropriate. Defendant filed this motion without providing any explanation for its decision to not file a timely ANTI-SLAPP or providing any basis for the Court to exercise its discretion to allow a late filing. Additionally, while Defendant's motion heavily relies on Sutter, decided prior to significant change in ANTI-SLAPP law, there is no discussion of Baral and its progeny on applicability of Sutter. Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376. The Court does not suggest that Sutter is no longer good law. However, Sutter's reliance on "gravamen" of the cause of action warranted a discussion. See Rutter-Guide, Civil Procedure Before Trial 7:772. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Patrick Hormillosa, Esq., 415-362-2529, appearing for Moises Mongalo, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Mark Wilson, Esq. of Manning & Kass, Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP, 415-400-0829, appearing for Walter Worsham for Defendant and moving party.

ARGUED. Mr. Wilson stated that court requested supplemental briefing, plaintiff cited South Sutter case and that case supports defendants position. Mr. Hormillosa reported that he does not contest to the tentative and that anti-slapp is moot.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MARSHALL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. South Sutter, LLC v. LJ Sutter Partners, L.P. (2011) 193 Cal.App.4th 634. Plaintiff is not prohibited from filing this action. Defendant may file an anti-SLAPP motion if appropriate.
Order for the same signed in open court.. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2019 Continued To Apr-12-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MARSHALL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: continued to April 12, 2019. By no later than March 29, 2019 Plaintiff shall file a supplemental brief listing any cases that are factually similar to the case at bar, specifically a dismissal of an action without prejudice in the face of a pending ANTI-SLAPP followed by filing of a new complaint, including the same causes of action and excluding "slappable" allegations. Plaintiff may accompany each citation by a one paragraph (not to exceed 20 lines) explanation/argument addressing why the case is on point and factually similar. If Plaintiff fails to locate any such cases, the supplemental brief should state so. By no later than April 8, 2019 Defendant may file a supplemental response brief addressing the cases cited by Plaintiff, limiting the argument to one paragraph (not to exceed 20 lines) per case. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

payments.description: STIP AND ORDER OR CONTINUANCE NOT REQUIRING HEARNG
